I performed the update in one go, but the correct approach should be to go through all DB releases step by step? For example:
```bash
sh manage_db.sh upgrade 21.01  
sh manage_db.sh upgrade 21.05  
....  
sh manage_db.sh upgrade 24.1
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/2418742355/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

My problem has been solved. Here is the solution.

1. I noticed that the "history_audit" database table was added in the release_21.05 version (#11914). So I cloned the release_21.05 repo and then built the `.venv` environment.

2. After the `.venv` environment and requirements are installed, execute `sh manage_db.sh upgrade` to successfully upgrade the postgresql database from 20.09 to 21.05. At this point, the newly added "history_audit" database table can be seen in the upgraded data backend.

3. Finally, upgrade the database from version 21.05 to version 24.0.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/2421068598/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
